meaning: adj.鍙互鍚冪殑n.椋熺墿,椋熷搧
examples: Though there are more eatable plants, there may also be wild animals, poisonous snakes, and other hazards.
David Edwards, a scientist at Harvard University, designed an eatable bag which can protect the food or drink inside.
Is five seconds on the floor the critical threshold that separates a piece of eatable food from a case of food poisoning?
